articles.title: Periscope Partners Invests in Amusement Connect, Backing FEC Tech Platform
articles.summary: Private equity firm Periscope Partners has made a strategic investment in Amusement Connect, a technology platform serving the amusement and FEC industry. The deal signals growing investor confidence in software and operational tech solutions tailored to coin-op and redemption-based entertainment venues. FEC operators should watch this space as consolidation and investment in industry-specific tech accelerates.

articles.title: FACE Amusement Group Opens 50,000 Sq Ft 'Guy Fieri's Downtown Flavortown' FEC in Myrtle Beach
articles.summary: FACE Amusement Group has launched a 50,000-square-foot dining and entertainment destination at Myrtle Beach Mall in partnership with celebrity chef Guy Fieri, building on the success of its Pigeon Forge flagship. The venue blends F&B and entertainment in a high-profile coastal market, illustrating the continued trend of celebrity-branded FEC concepts. Operators and investors should note how IP-driven entertainment-dining hybrids are expanding into secondary tourist markets.

articles.title: Location-Based Entertainment Market Continues Global Boom, New Report Finds
articles.summary: A new market report highlights that the location-based entertainment sector is experiencing strong worldwide growth, driven by consumer demand for out-of-home experiences. The findings are relevant for FEC operators and investors evaluating expansion opportunities and capital allocation. Understanding macro-level market momentum can help FEC businesses make informed decisions about new builds, renovations, and attraction investments.

articles.title: Urban Air Adventure Park Expands into Maryland, Continuing Nationwide Growth Push
articles.summary: Urban Air Adventure Park is opening a new indoor entertainment location in Maryland, adding to its rapidly growing national footprint. The franchise brand continues to be one of the most aggressive expanders in the FEC sector, targeting underserved suburban markets. Competing operators and prospective franchisees should monitor Urban Air's site selection strategy and consumer response in new markets.

articles.title: Salem Gets New Urban Air Adventure Park — Sneak Peek Inside
articles.summary: A new Urban Air Adventure Park has opened in Salem, Oregon, offering a first look at the brand's latest facility design and attraction mix. The opening reinforces Urban Air's strategy of entering mid-size markets with large-format indoor adventure venues. FEC operators in similar markets can study this rollout for competitive benchmarking and consumer demand signals.

articles.title: Tour the New Urban Air Adventure Park in Salem, Oregon
articles.summary: A walkthrough tour of the newly opened Urban Air Adventure Park in Salem gives a detailed look at the venue's attractions and layout. This provides useful comparative data for FEC operators considering similar builds or renovations. The coverage highlights the breadth of activities Urban Air is deploying in its newer locations.

articles.title: Red Raion Launches 'Dragon's Gold – The Chase' in Multiple Formats for FEC and Attraction Venues
articles.summary: CGI studio Red Raion has released Dragon's Gold – The Chase, a new licensable film available in 5D, VR, dome, and flying theatre formats, designed for family audiences and high-throughput venues. The title emphasizes repeatable experiences, synchronized physical effects, and flexible format deployment, making it easy for FEC operators to slot into existing media-based attractions. This type of plug-and-play content refresh is a low-cost way for venues to drive repeat visitation.

articles.title: Brogent Unveils R-Ride 360° Motion Theatre at IAAPA Expo Asia, Announces Five Southeast Asian Projects
articles.summary: Brogent Technologies debuted the R-Ride at IAAPA Expo Asia 2026, a motion theatre featuring a 360-degree panoramic screen and full rotation system capable of 8 RPM, delivering immersive monster, storm, and action sequences. The company also announced five new projects for Southeast Asia and a partnership with a major Japanese IP holder. For FEC operators eyeing media-based attractions, the R-Ride represents a next-generation option that could differentiate venues competing with traditional ride simulators.

articles.title: Brogent's R-Ride and Japanese IP Alliances Signal New Era for Media-Based Attractions
articles.summary: Amusement Today's coverage of Brogent's IAAPA Expo Asia showcase provides additional detail on the R-Ride system and the company's expanding IP partnerships with Japanese brands. The five Southeast Asian project announcements indicate strong regional demand for immersive ride theatres. Suppliers and FEC developers should evaluate whether this format is viable for smaller, non-theme-park entertainment venues.

articles.title: Plymouth, UK to Get Major New Go-Kart and Laser Tag Entertainment Hub
articles.summary: A new large-scale indoor entertainment venue featuring go-karts and laser tag is planned for Plymouth, England, adding to the growing wave of multi-activity FEC openings across the UK. The project reflects the continued strength of the indoor entertainment market in mid-size UK cities. Operators in the go-kart and laser tag space should track this development as evidence of sustained consumer demand for these core FEC activities.

articles.title: Tenpin Opens New Entertainment Centre in Blackpool, UK
articles.summary: Tenpin, the UK-based bowling and entertainment chain, has opened a new venue in Blackpool, continuing its expansion across British leisure markets. The opening reinforces Tenpin's strategy of pairing bowling with broader entertainment offerings to capture family and group audiences. FEC operators can look to Tenpin's rollout model as a case study in multi-activity venue development within retail and high-street locations.

articles.title: Multi-Million-Pound FEC Opens at Brent Cross Shopping Centre in London
articles.summary: A new multi-million-pound entertainment venue has opened at the Brent Cross retail and leisure destination in London, signaling continued investment in experiential entertainment anchors within shopping centers. Retail-adjacent FEC openings are a growing trend as mall operators seek to replace traditional retail with high-footfall experience venues. This development is relevant for FEC developers and operators exploring shopping center partnerships.

articles.title: WildBrain CPLG Tapped to Expand Miraculous Location-Based Entertainment Across EMEA and APAC
articles.summary: WildBrain CPLG has been appointed to develop location-based entertainment opportunities for the Miraculous franchise across Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and Asia-Pacific. The deal opens the door for FEC operators and entertainment venue developers in those regions to license one of animation's most popular current IP properties. Operators evaluating IP-themed experiences for their venues should take note of this licensing opportunity.

articles.title: Miraculous Corp Broadens Location-Based Entertainment Push with Expanded WildBrain CPLG Deal
articles.summary: Miraculous Corp has extended its partnership with WildBrain CPLG to cover location-based entertainment licensing across EMEA and APAC, making the Miraculous brand available for FEC and themed venue activations. This is a notable opportunity for operators in international markets looking to leverage a family-friendly, globally recognized animated IP. The move reflects a broader trend of animation studios actively courting the LBE sector.

articles.title: Madison, MS 'The Gallery' Entertainment District to Break Ground This Summer
articles.summary: A new entertainment district called The Gallery is set to break ground in Madison, Mississippi this summer, representing a significant development investment in the local leisure market. Entertainment district projects of this scale often incorporate FEC anchor tenants such as bowling, arcades, and dining concepts. Developers and operators tracking greenfield development opportunities in the Southeast should follow this project's progress.

articles.title: NRG Adventure Park Moves Forward at Jacksonville's Southside Commons
articles.summary: NRG Adventure Park is advancing its development at the Southside Commons mixed-use project in Jacksonville, Florida, adding an attraction-oriented anchor to the development. The project illustrates the growing role of adventure and FEC concepts as retail center anchors in the Southeast. FEC investors and operators expanding in Florida should monitor this development for competitive and partnership implications.

articles.title: Raleigh Urban Air Forced to Close Early After Riot Involving Three Women
articles.summary: A Raleigh, North Carolina Urban Air location was shut down early after a physical altercation escalated into a riot, resulting in three women being charged by police. The incident raises important questions about crowd management, security staffing, and incident response protocols for FEC operators. Venues with open-access policies for teens and young adults should review their security procedures in light of this event.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Offers Free Adventure Zone Admission on International Day of Play, Launches June Fundraiser
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese partnered with The Toy Foundation to offer free Adventure Zone admission on International Day of Play and launched a June fundraising campaign. The promotion demonstrates how FEC brands are using cause-related marketing and community events to drive foot traffic and brand goodwill. Operators can draw inspiration from this model for community engagement and off-peak traffic generation strategies.

articles.title: Play Playground Nashville Launches Monthly Cocktail Program to Drive Bar Revenue
articles.summary: Play Playground Nashville has introduced a monthly cocktail program called Playmaker's Pour, featuring patriotic-themed drinks for June and July to complement its social gaming experience. The initiative highlights how adult-oriented FECs are using F&B programming to increase per-capita spend and repeat visits. Operators of social entertainment venues with liquor licenses should consider how rotating beverage programs can create additional marketing hooks.

articles.title: London-Born Social Entertainment Venue with Foosball, VR, and Cocktails Coming to Paris
articles.summary: A London-based indoor entertainment concept featuring foosball, virtual soccer, interactive games, cocktails, and fast food is expanding to Paris, marking an international step for the social entertainment format. The concept blends competitive socializing with F&B in a format familiar to FEC operators worldwide. European FEC operators should watch this cross-border expansion as social gaming venues increasingly compete for the same adult leisure audience.

articles.title: Bear River Entertainment Zone in Northern California Draws Business Community Interest
articles.summary: Chamber of commerce members from Fortuna and Eureka visited the new Bear River entertainment zone, signaling strong local business interest in the new FEC-style venue. Destination entertainment zones linked to tribal or resort properties represent a growing development model in smaller markets. Operators and developers considering secondary market builds can look to this project as a regional demand case study.

articles.title: Sabre Lanes in Menasha Closes, Redirecting Customers to Appleton Bowling Center
articles.summary: Sabre Lanes in Menasha, Wisconsin has permanently closed, with ownership directing customers to an affiliated bowling center in nearby Appleton. The closure highlights ongoing consolidation pressures facing standalone bowling alleys in smaller markets. FEC operators in the bowling segment should monitor how rising operational costs and changing consumer habits are accelerating venue closures among single-activity operators.

articles.title: Floyd Bowling & Amusement Center Spotlighted as a Serious Bowling Business in Rome, GA
articles.summary: A profile of Floyd Bowling & Amusement Center in Rome, Georgia highlights how the venue has built a strong local following around competitive bowling alongside its broader amusement offerings. The story is a useful example of how small-market FECs are leveraging leagues and serious bowlers as a stable revenue base. Operators looking to retain core bowling customers while expanding entertainment offerings will find the model instructive.

articles.title: Frostbite's Arcade & Creamery Relocates from Holts Summit to Fulton, Missouri
articles.summary: Frostbite's Arcade & Creamery, a small-market FEC combining arcade gaming and ice cream, has relocated from Holts Summit to nearby Fulton, Missouri. The move reflects the challenges and opportunities facing independent FEC operators when evaluating site selection and market fit in small towns. Independent operators can draw lessons from this relocation about the importance of foot traffic, visibility, and community size when choosing or changing locations.

articles.title: Christie Launches XP Series LED with 10-Year Warranty for Immersive Entertainment Venues
articles.summary: Christie has introduced the XP Series LED display system, targeting immersive entertainment spaces and mission-critical environments with a 10-year warranty and advanced color fidelity powered by its VividLife platform. The long warranty and commercial-grade reliability specs make this a noteworthy option for FEC operators building out VR arenas, projection-mapped spaces, or high-end arcade environments. Suppliers and venue designers specifying display technology for new FEC builds should evaluate this platform.

articles.title: ProSlide Debuts PowerRAPIDS, a New Category of Water Ride, at Vietnam's Aquatopia
articles.summary: ProSlide Technology has unveiled PowerRAPIDS, a first-of-its-kind water ride format that integrates floating channels with traditional sliding sections to simulate a rushing-river experience. The global debut at Aquatopia in Vietnam marks a new product category for the manufacturer, with implications for water park operators seeking differentiated, high-throughput attractions. FEC operators with water park components or those planning water entertainment additions should track this new format's consumer response.

articles.title: Expert Guide to Building Modular Travelling Exhibitions for Global Touring
articles.summary: A detailed guide from five global touring exhibition experts covers best practices in modular design, logistics, sustainability, and operational documentation for travelling exhibitions. While focused on museums and IP holders, the frameworks for scalable, repeatable attraction design have direct relevance for FEC operators running rotating or travelling experiences. Key takeaways include using commercial off-the-shelf components, stackable tour carts, and remote-updatable digital assets to minimize downtime and cost.

articles.title: Merlin Expands Minecraft Collaboration with UK Pop-Up Experience at Alton Towers
articles.summary: Merlin Entertainments is bringing a Minecraft Meet the Mobs pop-up experience to Alton Towers this summer, featuring life-size baby mob characters, interactive activities, themed snacks, and exclusive in-game rewards. The experience demonstrates how gaming IP can be activated within physical entertainment venues to drive incremental visits and merchandise revenue. FEC operators should note the model of limited-time IP pop-ups as a low-risk way to refresh their programming calendar.
